commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3e2b213e41d7a5b2e0e2a39769e534c605ab1a2d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves accumulated partial assistant content when the stream iterator throws mid-stream and adds agent-loop tests for text/thinking partial content recovery.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +199. Total +228 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, but not with a live runtime run in this read-only review. Current main emits transient partial updates, persists only terminal `message_end` messages, and falls back to an empty assistant failure message when stream processing rejects before terminal finalization.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor handling is needed because the branch needs a narrow session-state repair and the missing real behavior proof cannot be supplied by repair automation.

target.title: fix(agent-core): preserve partial streaming content on stream iterator error
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When an LLM streaming response is interrupted mid-stream by an idle timeout
or transport abort, the text content that was already streamed and visible
in the UI is permanently lost. After a page refresh, the conversation shows
no visible assistant content â€” only the timeout error text.

### Root Cause

In `streamAssistantResponse` (`packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.ts`),
streaming text chunks arrive as events and are accumulated in the
`partialMessage` variable, then delivered to the UI via `message_update`.
However, `message_update` events are **never persisted** to the session
file â€” only `message_end` triggers persistence (`agent-session.ts:637`,
`sessionManager.appendMessage`).

When the stream stalls and the idle timeout watchdog fires
(`streamWithIdleTimeout`), the `Promise.race` between
`streamIterator.next()` and the timeout promise rejects, interrupting the
`for await` loop **before** `message_end` is emitted for `partialMessage`.
The variable goes out of scope and all accumulated text is discarded.

The error propagates up: `streamAssistantResponse` â†’ `runLoop` â†’
`runAgentLoop` â†’ `runWithLifecycle` â†’ `handleRunFailure` (agent.ts:535).

`handleRunFailure` synthesizes a **new failure message with empty content**
(`[{ type: "text", text: "" }]`, `stopReason: "aborted"`). This empty
message is what gets persisted to the session file, replacing the real
partial content that was already shown to the user.

### Fix

Wrap the `for await` loop and post-loop fallback in `try/catch` inside
`streamAssistantResponse`. When the stream iterator throws and
`partialMessage` has been populated (a `start` event was received with
at least one content block), the catch block:

1. Emits `message_end` with `partialMessage` (preserving the real
   streamed content), `stopReason: "error"`, and the original error
   in `errorMessage`.
2. Returns the terminal message **instead of rethrowing**, so `runLoop`
   processes it through its normal `stopReason === "error"` /
   `stopReason === "aborted"` turn-end path. This avoids
   `handleRunFailure` emitting a duplicate empty-content message.

When `partialMessage` is not populated (error before any content), the
error is still rethrown so `handleRunFailure` can synthesize the
appropriate error payload.

### Behavior Change

| Scenario | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Idle timeout after partial streaming | Session has empty content + error text | Session has real partial content + error metadata |
| Idle timeout before any streaming | `handleRunFailure` creates error message | Unchanged (error rethrown) |
| Normal completion (`done`/`error` via provider) | Unchanged | Unchanged |
| Upper-layer failover / retry logic | Unchanged | Unchanged |

## User Impact

- Users no longer lose partial streaming content on idle timeout.
  Refreshing the page after a timeout shows the text the model produced
  before stalling.
- The `stopReason` is `"error"` so the UI can display an appropriate
  error state alongside the preserved content.
- The original timeout error message is preserved in `errorMessage`.
- No impact on normal completion paths, failover, retry, or model
  fallback logic.

## Evidence

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| TypeScript (`pnpm tsgo`) | âś… Clean |
| Build (`pnpm build`) | âś… Clean, no dynamic import issues |
| agent-core tests (56 tests, 11 files) | âś… All passing |
| agent-loop tests (18 tests, incl. 3 new) | âś… All passing |
| Formatting (`oxfmt --check`) | âś… Clean |

### New Test Cases (3 added)

1. **`emits message_end with partial content when the stream iterator
   throws mid-stream`** â€” Verifies that text content from `text_delta`
   events is preserved in the terminal message when the async iterator
   throws after streaming has started.

2. **`propagates the error when no partial content was received before
   the stream iterator throws`** â€” Verifies that errors before any
   content still propagate to `handleRunFailure` unchanged.

3. **`preserves multi-block partial content (text + thinking) on
   mid-stream error`** â€” Verifies that both thinking and text content
   blocks accumulated across multiple stream events are preserved in
   the terminal message.
